leadText: Tympole (Japanese: オタマロ Otamaro) is a Water-type Pokémon introduced in Generation V. It evolves into Palpitoad starting at level 25, which evolves into Seismitoad starting at level 36.

sections.title: Biology
sections.text: Biology [ edit | edit source ] Tympole is a round, tadpole-like Pokémon with a tan, circular face. It has large, oval, black eyes; a wide mouth with down-turned corners; and black eyebrows in the shape of quarter notes. Its spherical body is black with no visible limbs. Instead, it has two black-and-blue ringed cheeks akin to half-spheres on either side of its head. It has a long, blue tail with a fan-like shape and a black spot at its base. Tympole vibrates its cheeks to create a sound too high-pitched for humans and other species of Pokémon to hear. The sounds are used in rhythm to communicate with others of its kind, particularly for warning of danger. They are known to sing in high-pitched voices, which creates graceful ripples on the water's surface. The Pokémon the Series: Black & White episode Facing Fear with Eyes Wide Open! reveals that Tympole swim and attack in groups. Evolution [ edit | edit source ] Tympole evolves into Palpitoad , which evolves into Seismitoad . (For specifics on this Pokémon's Evolution in the games, refer to Game data→Evolution data .) Unevolved Tympole Water → First Evolution Palpitoad Water Ground → Second Evolution Seismitoad Water Ground

sections.title: Pokédex entries
sections.text: Pokédex entries [ edit | edit source ] Manga Chapter Entry Pokémon Adventures PS468 Tympole emits rhythmic subsonic sound waves by vibrating its cheeks. These waves can be used to communicate.
